name: Cold Rhubarb Souffle
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook the rhubarb with 1/4 cup water and 1 3/4 cups sugar in a heavy-bottomed saucepan for about 10 minutes until soft. Strain and cook down the juice to 1/2 cup. Puree the rhubarb in a food mill or blender, or through a vegetable mill.
2. Soften the gelatin in 2 tablespoons cold water, then add it to the hot rhubarb juice and stir until completely dissolved. Add the puree.
3. Beat the cream until stiff.
4. Beat the egg whites until they begin to stiffen, then add the remaining 1/2 cup sugar and vanilla, continuing to beat until stiff peaks form.
5. Fold first the egg white mixture into the rhubarb, then the whipped cream. Make a collar of wax paper and fit it around a 1 1/2 quart souffle mold. Chill for at least six hours. Remove the collar and decorate the top with fresh strawberries and rosettes of whipped cream piped from a pastry tube.
